mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0%, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous housing starts expansion and industrial production strength signal broad demand recovery, lifting corporate earnings expectations and S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 10 units while INDPRO exceeds 95
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing, or SPX 20-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion regimes, rising industrial production signals capacity constraints and inflationary pressure, prompting rate hike expectations that compress equity multiples over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows positive 5-day forward returns in over 60% of rising INDPRO instances, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when adjusted for CPIAUCSL momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth acceleration directly increases corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly higher during GDP acceleration periods compared to contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI acceleration above 0.3 percent triggers inflation fears and anticipated Fed tightening, increasing discount rates and depressing equity valuations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Joint PAYEMS growth exceeding 200k and UNRATE declining simultaneously confirms robust labor demand, reducing recession risk premiums and lifting S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to significantly predict returns in a multivariate regression, or SPX 5-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both conditions align.

mechanism_narrative: During credit expansion regimes, rising RSAFS indicates accommodative financing conditions that boost housing demand and related consumer spending, supporting equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly higher during RSAFS acceleration periods compared to deceleration periods.
